shortDescription: For charitable/religious trusts, political parties, educational institutions, and other entities claiming exemption u/s 11/12 or 10(23C)
longDescription: ITR-7 is filed by persons who are required to file return u/s 139(4A), 139(4B), 139(4C), or 139(4D). This covers: (1) Charitable and religious trusts registered u/s 12A or 12AB claiming exemption u/s 11; (2) Political parties registered with Election Commission filing u/s 139(4B); (3) Research associations, hospitals, educational institutions u/s 139(4C); (4) Universities/institutions approved u/s 10(23C) filing u/s 139(4D).

eligibilityCriteria.detail: Public charitable or religious trust or society registered u/s 12A (old registration) or 12AB (new registration from FY 2021-22). Registration must be valid in FY 2025-26.
Political parties registered with Election Commission of India and maintaining accounts as per section 13A. Must file ITR-7 even if income is fully exempt.
Educational institutions, hospitals, research associations approved under specific provisions of 10(23C). File u/s 139(4C) or 139(4D).
Association of Persons or Body of Individuals that is a registered charitable institution uses ITR-7 (not ITR-5).

exclusionCriteria.detail: Unregistered trusts cannot claim u/s 11 exemption and should file ITR-5 (if firm-type AOP) or ITR-6 (if company).
exclusionCriteria.consequence: No charitable exemption; full income taxed

exclusionCriteria.detail: ITR-7 is not for individual taxpayers. Individuals filing returns use ITR-1 through ITR-3.

commonMisconceptions: Misconception: Registered trust's income is always tax-free. Reality: Only if at least 85% of income is applied for charitable purposes (or set apart u/s 11(2)). Shortfall is taxed at 30%.
Misconception: Political parties don't need to file ITR. Reality: Political parties must file ITR-7 even if fully exempt — non-filing attracts penalties and can affect exemption status.
Misconception: Corpus fund contributions are income. Reality: Corpus fund (specific donation towards corpus) is a capital receipt, not income, and is NOT subject to the 85% application requirement.
Misconception: 12A registration is permanent. Reality: From FY 2022-23, all trusts must have renewed registration u/s 12AB. Old 12A registrations were grandfathered but should be renewed.
fy202526Changes: Periodic re-registration every 5 years mandatory under 12AB — trusts registered in 2020-21 must check renewal status
Form 10B (audit report for trusts with income > ₹1Cr) — compliance tightened
CBDT requires Form 10BD (donor reporting) and Form 10BE (donor certificate) by May 31, 2026 for donations received in FY 2025-26
